questions.question: Draw a circle of radius $3 \text{ cm}$. Take two points $P$ and $Q$ on one of its extended diameters each at a distance of $7 \text{ cm}$ from its centre. Draw tangents to the circle from these two points $P$ and $Q$.
questions.answer: Two pairs of tangents constructed from points at distance $7 \text{ cm}$

steps: Draw a circle with center $O$ and radius $3 \text{ cm}$.
Draw a diameter and extend it on both sides to locate points $P$ and $Q$ such that $OP = OQ = 7 \text{ cm}$.
Bisect $OP$ and $OQ$ to find midpoints $M_1$ and $M_2$.
With $M_1$ and $M_2$ as centers, draw circles of radius $M_1O$ to intersect the original circle at two points each. Join $P$ and $Q$ to these intersection points.
solution.derivation: Construction follows the geometric property: angle in a semicircle is $90^\circ$.

questions.question: Construct a tangent to a circle of radius $4 \text{ cm}$ from a point on the concentric circle of radius $6 \text{ cm}$ and measure its length.

steps: Draw two concentric circles with radii $4 \text{ cm}$ and $6 \text{ cm}$ with center $O$.
Mark a point $P$ on the circumference of the larger circle.
Join $OP$ and bisect it to find midpoint $M$.
Draw a circle with center $M$ and radius $MO$ to cut the inner circle at $A$ and $B$. $PA$ and $PB$ are the required tangents.

questions.question: Draw a line segment $AB$ of length $8 \text{ cm}$. Taking $A$ as center, draw a circle of radius $4 \text{ cm}$ and taking $B$ as center, draw another circle of radius $3 \text{ cm}$. Construct tangents to each circle from the center of the other circle.

steps: Draw segment $AB = 8 \text{ cm}$. Draw circle $C_1$ (center $A$, $r=4$) and $C_2$ (center $B$, $r=3$).
Bisect $AB$ to get midpoint $M$.
Draw a circle with center $M$ and radius $MA$ (or $MB$).
The points where this circle intersects $C_1$ and $C_2$ are the points of tangency. Join $B$ to intersection points on $C_1$ and $A$ to intersection points on $C_2$.
solution.derivation: Tangents are constructed by finding the intersection of circles centered at the midpoint of the line segment connecting the centers.

questions.question: Draw a circle of radius $3.5 \text{ cm}$. Construct a pair of tangents to this circle which are inclined to each other at an angle of $60^\circ$.
questions.answer: Construction of tangents at $120^\circ$ central angle

steps: Draw a circle of radius $3.5 \text{ cm}$ with center $O$.
Draw a radius $OA$. Draw another radius $OB$ such that $\angle AOB = 120^\circ$ (since $180^\circ - 60^\circ = 120^\circ$).
At point $A$, draw a perpendicular to $OA$.
At point $B$, draw a perpendicular to $OB$. The intersection point $P$ gives the required tangents $PA$ and $PB$.
solution.derivation: Sum of opposite angles in the quadrilateral $OAPB$ is $180^\circ$. Thus, if $\angle APB = 60^\circ$, $\angle AOB = 120^\circ$.

questions.question: Construct a triangle with sides $5 \text{ cm}, 6 \text{ cm}$ and $7 \text{ cm}$. Then construct a circle touching the side of $6 \text{ cm}$ at its midpoint and passing through the other vertex.
questions.answer: Construction of a circle through specific vertices tangent to a side

steps: Construct $\triangle ABC$ with $AB=5, BC=6, AC=7$.
Locate midpoint $M$ of $BC$.
Draw a perpendicular to $BC$ at $M$.
Draw the perpendicular bisector of $AM$. The intersection of these two perpendiculars is the center $O$. Draw the circle with radius $OM$.
solution.derivation: The center must be equidistant from the point of tangency $M$ and vertices $A$ and $C$.
